description_long: This object is a heavy, stationary, twin-barreled automated turret with a robust, military-grade design. Its main body is constructed from olive drab metal panels, featuring two large cylindrical barrels mounted side by side, each with a reinforced muzzle. The turret is supported by a wide, four-legged base that provides stability and suggests it is intended for fixed emplacement rather than portability. The central housing contains what appear to be targeting optics, control panels, and possibly sensors, all encased in armored plating. The rear section is boxy and solid, with minimal detailing, while the sides reveal the full length of the barrels and the mounting hardware. The overall appearance is utilitarian and functional, designed for durability and effectiveness in urban defense or security scenarios.
description: A heavy, twin-barreled automated turret with an armored olive drab body and a wide, four-legged base, designed for stationary defense.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin barrels, targeting optics, and armored central housing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the barrels, mounting hardware, and the sturdy four-legged base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid, boxy back panel and the symmetrical leg arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the barrels and the robust support structure.
